title: Civic Kernel stress-test scenario
description: One imagined real-life human situation walked through the protocol. Test-harness format, not part of the waist. The embedded manifest and log-entry artifacts must additionally validate against the two waist schemas in ../schema/ — that cross-check is the test: can the waist express this situation? Enforced by tools/validate.py.

setting.description: The scene: named people, a real place, what is at stake. General-reader prose.

precedents.description: Documented real-world events the fiction is modeled on: the people are invented, the attack pattern is not. Same discipline the kernel demands of a form field (no demand without a cited rule): no attack without a cited precedent.

event.description: Short name of the documented event, case, or statistic.

source.description: Publisher or institution behind the url.

shows.description: One sentence: which of this scenario's attack patterns the event documents.

manifest.description: The community's conformance manifest; must validate against ../schema/manifest.schema.json.

verb.description: One of the four citizen verbs (§8; cast/challenge split), or 'offstage' for the parts of life the kernel deliberately does not touch.

artifact.description: The log entry this step appends, if any; must validate against ../schema/log-entry.schema.json.
